note: Pre-departure calibration. Aircraft at gate. Known position. Use for BTO bias calibration.
Shortly after takeoff. BTO implies ~2305 NM from satellite - consistent with KUL position.
Last ACARS. BFO only - no BTO available for this message type. Position from ACARS: 5.27N 102.79E

SDU reboot after power cycle. BFO unreliable due to oscillator warm-up transient (Holland 2017 Sec V). First in settling sequence: 142, 273, 176, 175, 172, 144, 143 Hz. Low C/N0 and non-zero BER - deemed untrustworthy by SATCOM working group. BFO=142 Hz from joewragg/MH370 FinalData.csv and radiantphysics.com.

7 seconds after reboot request. BFO dominated by OCXO oscillator warm-up transient (Holland 2017 Sec V). +131 Hz change in 7s is anomalous - no plausible maneuver explains this without warm-up transient. BFO=273 Hz from joewragg/MH370 FinalData.csv. BTO here is inconsistent with 18:25:27 value.

First clean data point with both BTO and BFO. BFO=111 Hz from joewragg/MH370 FinalData.csv. Lowest BFO in the hourly sequence - consistent with aircraft heading nearly perpendicular to satellite LOS at this point.
BFO=141 Hz from joewragg/MH370 FinalData.csv. BFO rising from Arc 2 minimum - aircraft LOS velocity toward satellite increasing as it tracks further south.
BFO=168 Hz from joewragg/MH370 FinalData.csv. BFO continuing to rise as aircraft moves further south.
BFO=204 Hz from joewragg/MH370 FinalData.csv. BFO continues rising monotonically.

Malaysia Airlines attempted to call aircraft. Triggered a transmission. BTO very similar to arc 5 - aircraft moved very little in 33 minutes. Either speed reduced significantly, or heading changed to more tangential to satellite.
BFO=252 Hz from joewragg/MH370 FinalData.csv. Highest BFO in the hourly sequence. Linear trend from Arc 2 (111 Hz) to here (252 Hz) extrapolates to ~254 Hz at 00:19Z (Holland 2017 Fig 5).

SDU reboot triggered by APU auto-start after engine flameout. BTO identical to arc 6 - aircraft barely moved in 8.5 minutes. BFO here may already be affected by descent/dive. This defines THE 7TH ARC.

BFO=-2 Hz from Holland 2017 Table III. 8 seconds after 00:19:29 (182 Hz). Drop of 184 Hz implies rapid descent at 2,900-14,800 fpm (Holland Table IV/VII). Also affected by OCXO warm-up drift: recorded BFO was 17-130 Hz higher than steady-state (Holland Sec V-B). This is the LAST TRANSMISSION.

In-Flight Entertainment system should have sent a setup ping ~90 seconds after SDU reboot. It was never received. Four possible explanations: (a) IFE manually switched off from cockpit, (b) IFE/SDU lost power due to APU flameout, (c) APU ran out of fuel before IFE initialized, (d) aircraft already impacted. The absence of this ping is used to argue aircraft hit water before 00:21. But explanation (a) - deliberate pilot action - cannot be ruled out.
